categories.name: File Reference Validation
categories.description: Validates that file paths referenced in context files exist in the project.

categories.name: Build/Script Validation
categories.description: Validates that build and test commands referenced in context files match project scripts.

categories.name: Freshness Detection
categories.description: Detects context files that haven't been updated while referenced code has changed.

categories.name: Context Window Budget
categories.description: Monitors context file size to help teams manage context window consumption.

categories.name: Tier-Aware Token Accounting
categories.description: Reports per-section token cost for always-loaded context files and flags demotion candidates for on-demand tiers (skills, subagents, memory).

categories.name: Inferable Content
categories.description: Detects content that agents can already infer from project metadata.

categories.name: Cross-File Conflicts
categories.description: Detects conflicting directives across multiple context files.

categories.name: Client Metadata Validation
categories.description: Validates YAML frontmatter required by specific AI coding clients.

categories.name: CI Workflow Documentation
categories.description: Checks that release/deploy CI workflows are documented in context files.

categories.name: CI Secrets Documentation
categories.description: Checks that CI secrets used in workflows are documented in context files.

categories.name: Hook Enforcement Coverage
categories.description: Validates that PreToolUse hooks and permissions entries in .claude/settings.json reference scripts that exist on disk. The inverse of tier-tokens/hard-enforcement-missing.

categories.name: Inline Secret Detection
categories.description: Detects inline-pasted secrets (API keys, tokens, private-key headers) in context files. Complements mcp-security, which covers MCP config files; this covers the context-file paste surface (CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, .cursorrules, etc.).

rules.description: Referenced file or directory does not exist at the specified path.
rules.trigger: Path reference in context file resolves to a non-existent file or directory.
rules.fixDescription: Replace broken path with suggestion from git rename detection or fuzzy matching.

rules.description: Glob pattern matches zero files in the project.
rules.trigger: Path contains wildcard characters (*, ?) but matches no files.

rules.description: Referenced directory does not exist.
rules.trigger: Path ending with / does not resolve to an existing directory.

rules.description: Package manager script name is not found in package.json scripts.
rules.trigger: npm run, pnpm, yarn, or bun script reference does not match a key in package.json#scripts.

rules.description: Make target is not found in Makefile.
rules.trigger: make command references a target not declared in the project's Makefile.

rules.description: Make command used but no Makefile exists in the project.
rules.trigger: Context file references a make command but no Makefile is found at the project root.

rules.description: npx package is not in project dependencies.
rules.trigger: npx package is not in package.json dependencies/devDependencies and not in node_modules/.bin.

rules.description: Common development tool is not in project dependencies.
rules.trigger: Tool name (vitest, jest, eslint, prettier, tsc, etc.) not found in dependencies or node_modules/.bin.
rules.message: "{cmd}" — "{tool}" not found in dependencies or node_modules/.bin

rules.description: package.json is missing or unparseable, so all script/npx/tool command validation silently skips. Emitted once per context file that contains at least one command reference that would otherwise have been validated, so the skip isn't invisible.
rules.trigger: package.json cannot be loaded AND the context file references at least one npm-run/shorthand/npx/tool command.
rules.message: package.json missing or unparseable — command checks skipped

rules.description: Context file has not been updated in 30+ days while referenced code has changed.
rules.trigger: File last modified 30+ days ago AND paths it references have git commits since.
rules.message: Last updated {days} days ago. {path} has {N} commits since.

rules.description: Context file has not been updated in 14-30 days while referenced code has changed.
rules.trigger: File last modified 14-30 days ago AND paths it references have git commits since.
rules.message: Last updated {days} days ago. {path} has {N} commits since.

rules.description: Single context file consumes excessive context window space.
rules.trigger: File uses 8000+ tokens (configurable).
rules.message: {N} tokens — consumes significant context window space

rules.description: Single context file is large.
rules.trigger: File uses 3000-7999 tokens (configurable).

rules.description: Reports per-file token count.
rules.trigger: File uses 1000+ tokens (configurable).

rules.description: Combined context files exceed recommended total token budget.
rules.trigger: All context files combined use 5000+ tokens (configurable) AND there are multiple files.

rules.description: Reports the heaviest top-level sections in an always-loaded context file and suggests demoting the largest to an on-demand tier (skill, subagent, or memory).
rules.trigger: An always-loaded context file (CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, .cursorrules, etc. — excluding path-scoped rules files) reaches or exceeds the tierBreakdown threshold (default 1000 tokens; the boundary is inclusive) and contains H1 or H2 sections.
rules.message: {N} tokens loaded every session — heaviest top-level section(s): ...

rules.description: Combined token cost of always-loaded context files exceeds the recommended budget.
rules.trigger: Two or more always-loaded files total the tierAggregate threshold (default 4000 tokens) or more; the boundary is inclusive.
rules.message: {count} always-loaded files total {N} tokens — loaded every session

rules.description: An always-loaded file uses inviolable framing (NEVER / ALWAYS / DO NOT / MUST NOT) paired with a concrete command, but no corresponding PreToolUse or Stop hook, permissions.deny entry, or permissions.ask entry exists in settings.json. Rules in always-loaded files are advisory — without a hook the agent may still run the command.
rules.trigger: Line in an always-loaded file matches NEVER|ALWAYS|DO NOT|MUST NOT with a backticked command, and the project's .claude/settings.json / .claude/settings.local.json does not contain a matching PreToolUse or Stop hook, permissions.deny entry, or permissions.ask entry (ask gates the command behind a human prompt, so it counts as enforcement). The user-global ~/.claude/settings.json is consulted only on explicit opt-in (the same gate as hook-coverage's --hooks-global).
rules.message: Inviolable framing without a hook to back it up

rules.description: Context file mentions a technology that is already in package.json dependencies.
rules.trigger: File contains phrases like 'use React' or 'built with Express' and that package is in dependencies.
rules.message: "{tech}" is in package.json {depType} — agent can infer this

rules.description: Context file describes the location of a directory that the agent can discover by listing files.
rules.trigger: File says files 'are in' or 'go in' a directory that exists in the project.
rules.message: Directory "{dir}" exists and is discoverable — agent can find this by listing files

rules.description: Two context files have significant content overlap.
rules.trigger: Two files share 60%+ of their lines (by trimmed content, lines >10 chars).

rules.description: Two context files specify mutually exclusive options in the same category.
rules.trigger: File A says 'use Jest' while File B says 'use Vitest' (or similar conflicts across 8 categories).

rules.description: File format requires or recommends frontmatter but none is present. Severity is per-format: warning for Cursor .mdc (frontmatter is required), info for Copilot instructions and Windsurf rules (frontmatter is optional/recommended).
rules.trigger: Cursor .mdc, Copilot instruction, or Windsurf rule file has no --- frontmatter block.

rules.description: A required or recommended frontmatter field is absent.
rules.trigger: Frontmatter exists but is missing a key field (description for Cursor, applyTo for Copilot, trigger for Windsurf).

rules.description: A frontmatter field has an invalid value. The error severity applies to the unambiguous branches (alwaysApply not a boolean, Windsurf trigger outside the enum); a possibly-malformed globs value (unbalanced brackets or quotes) is reported at warning severity because Cursor accepts bare directory/extension globs.
rules.trigger: alwaysApply is not true/false (error), trigger is not a valid enum value (error), or globs has unbalanced brackets/quotes (warning).

rules.description: Frontmatter opens with --- but is never closed, so the host loads the file with no frontmatter at all and every parsed field is suspect. Reported instead of frontmatter/missing because the user clearly tried to write frontmatter.
rules.trigger: File opens with a --- line but no matching closing --- line follows (typical cause: the closing fence typo'd as -- or ___, or indented).
rules.message: Frontmatter opens with `---` but is never closed

rules.description: File has frontmatter but no activation mechanism.
rules.trigger: Cursor .mdc has frontmatter with neither globs nor alwaysApply set.
rules.message: No activation field — rule may not be applied automatically

rules.description: Release/deploy CI workflow exists but no context file documents the process.
rules.trigger: .github/workflows/ contains release/deploy/publish workflow but no context file mentions the release process.
rules.message: Release workflow found but no context file documents the release process

rules.description: CI workflow references a secret not mentioned in any context file.
rules.trigger: ${{ secrets.NAME }} found in workflow YAML but NAME not mentioned in any context file. GitHub-provided secrets (GITHUB_TOKEN, etc.) are excluded.
rules.message: CI secret "{name}" is used in {workflow} but not mentioned in any context file

rules.description: A PreToolUse (or other) hook command, or a permissions entry, in .claude/settings.json references a script/path that does not exist on disk. The gate silently no-ops -- Claude Code cannot run a missing script, so the protection the user believes is in place does nothing.
rules.trigger: A hooks.<Event>[].hooks[].command or permissions.{allow,deny,ask}[] entry contains a path-shaped token (resolved through ~, $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR, $CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR, $HOME) that does not exist on disk. Entries with unresolvable env vars are skipped (cannot be verified). Scans project .claude/settings.json + settings.local.json; the user-global ~/.claude/settings.json is scanned only on explicit opt-in (--hooks-global).
rules.message: {origin} references "{path}" which does not exist on disk -- the gate silently no-ops

rules.description: A PEM private-key header is pasted into a context file. Context files usually end up committed to git, so an inline private key is a leak.
rules.trigger: A line contains -----BEGIN [RSA |EC |DSA |OPENSSH |PGP ]PRIVATE KEY-----. Lines containing placeholder tokens (example, redacted, xxxx, ...), commented fake/example lines, and explicitly illustrative code fences (text, txt, example, pseudocode, none) are suppressed.

rules.description: An AWS access key ID is pasted into a context file. Emitted messages never include the full secret — only a 6-char redacted prefix.
rules.trigger: AKIA (long-lived) or ASIA (STS temporary) prefix followed by 16 uppercase alphanumeric characters, outside placeholder/illustrative contexts.

rules.description: A GitHub token is pasted into a context file. Covers classic PATs (ghp_), fine-grained PATs (github_pat_), and server-to-server / OAuth / user / refresh tokens (ghs_ / gho_ / ghu_ / ghr_); the non-PAT variants are emitted with the label "GitHub token".
rules.trigger: ghp_{36,}, github_pat_{82,}, or gh[soru]_{36,} token shapes, outside placeholder/illustrative contexts.
rules.message: GitHub personal access token detected in {file} ({redacted-prefix}...)

rules.description: An Anthropic API key is pasted into a context file.
rules.trigger: sk-ant- prefix followed by 20+ key characters, outside placeholder/illustrative contexts. Checked before the OpenAI sk- pattern so the same substring isn't flagged twice.

rules.description: An OpenAI API key is pasted into a context file.
rules.trigger: sk- or sk-proj- prefix followed by 20+ key characters (the length floor avoids flagging sk-... ellipses in docs), outside placeholder/illustrative contexts.

rules.description: An npm automation token is pasted into a context file.
rules.trigger: npm_ prefix followed by 36+ alphanumeric characters, outside placeholder/illustrative contexts.

rules.description: A Slack token is pasted into a context file (bot, user, app, admin, or refresh token).
rules.trigger: xox[bpoasr]- prefix followed by 10+ token characters, outside placeholder/illustrative contexts.

rules.description: A Google API key is pasted into a context file.
rules.trigger: AIza prefix followed by exactly 35 key characters, outside placeholder/illustrative contexts.

rules.description: A Stripe live secret key is pasted into a context file.
rules.trigger: sk_live_ prefix followed by 24+ alphanumeric characters, outside placeholder/illustrative contexts.
rules.message: Stripe live secret key detected in {file} ({redacted-prefix}...)
